races.race_name: Racing TV Sky Channel 424 EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Margaret's Pearl
runners.spotlight: Belied 20-1 odds when keeping on for 2l fourth of eight on debut at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) a fortnight ago; could be sharper for that experience and today's stiff finish should suit; leading contender.
runners.comment: 2l fourth on debut at Wolverhampton and she's a leading contender this afternoon

runners.name: Sea Mirage
runners.spotlight: 25,000euros foal, £34,000 yearling; second foal; Naval Crown half-brother to 5f AW winner Dandy Breeze (RPR 78); dam placed 6.6f 2yo (69), from talented family; trainer saddled a well-backed 2yo newcomer to win at Thirsk recently; respected on debut, all the more so if strong in the betting.
runners.comment: Trainer saddled a well-backed 2yo newcomer to win at Thirsk recently; respected on debut

runners.name: Ubettanotblink
runners.spotlight: £10,000 yearling; fifth foal; closely related to 7f/1m winner Adace (RPR 66), half-brother to winners The Spotlight Kid (7f/1m; 74), Haveachatma (5f/6f AW; 71) and Equipped (6f AW; 70); dam 5.5f winner (82); this Ubettabelieveit debutant may be one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Related to several winners but this debutant may be one for further down the line

runners.name: Village Hall
runners.spotlight: 6,000gns yearling; second foal; dam unplaced 6f-1m (RPR 44), half-sister to 7f 2yo winner Borsdane Wood, out of unraced sister to dam of July Cup winner Mayson; it's likely this Tasleet newcomer will improve for the run.
runners.comment: 6,000gns yearling; dam unplaced; it's likely this newcomer will improve for the run

runners.name: Cydney Sweenie
runners.spotlight: 9,000gns foal, 25,000gns yearling; seventh foal; Caturra half-sister to winners including Vince Le Prince (7f including 2yo; RPR 83), Freyabella (5f AW 2yo; 82); dam French Group-placed 1m 2yo Flat; trainer's horses are running well and he can ready one first time; a possible.
runners.comment: Half-sister to a couple of useful winners; trainer can ready one first time

runners.name: Claret And Blues
runners.spotlight: 22,000gns yearling; 11th foal; Sergei Prokofiev brother to 5f 2yo winner Barrak (RPR 80), closely related to 6f winner Wichahpi (including 2yo; 78), half-brother to six winners including Twilight Madness (prolific 5f/6f; 92); bred to be quick and could have a big part to play on debut.
runners.comment: 22,000gns yearling; bred to be quick and could have a big part to play on debut

runners.name: Jolly Good Fellows
runners.spotlight: Kept on for 3l third of seven at 17-2 on debut at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) 11 days ago; her dam was a 5f and 6f (Listed) 2yo winner and there can be optimism that she'll improve; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Third on recent debut at Wolverhampton and there can be optimism that she'll improve

runners.name: Too Many Spirits
runners.spotlight: Bred to sprint and he was backed in to 13-2 when fifth of eight, beaten just over 8l, on recent debut at Thirsk (5f, good); it will be interesting to see if his backers return but he needs a sizeable step forward.
runners.comment: Backed into 13-2 on recent debut at Thirsk but was soundly beaten in fifth of eight

races.race_name: Constant Security Services Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Lady Youmzain
runners.spotlight: Hello Youmzain filly who created an excellent impression when making a winning debut in 5f Beverley novice in September, drawing clear last 1f; she looks to have plenty more to offer this year and is the one to beat.
runners.comment: Created an excellent impression when winning on debut at Beverley; the one to beat

runners.name: Elemma
runners.spotlight: 135,000gns yearling; half-sister to five winners, notably Justifier (7f/7.5f Listed; RPR 106) and The Man (5f; RPR 102); dam, 5f/6f winner (RPR 95), is a half-sister to St Leger winner Kew Gardens; this Starman filly can't be ruled out, especially if the betting speaks in her favour.
runners.comment: This Starman filly can't be ruled out, especially if the betting speaks in her favour

runners.name: Town Queen
runners.spotlight: 160,000euros yearling; half-sister to three winners, notably Raving Royal (12.4f; RPR 85); dam Canadian 6f and 8.5f (Grade 3) AW 2yo winner (RPR 99); in same ownership as Lady Youmzain but this daughter of Blue Point needs considering.
runners.comment: In same ownership as Lady Youmzain but this daughter of Blue Point needs considering

runners.name: Reader
runners.spotlight: Shaped well when a debut second in 6f novice at Goodwood but she came in only eighth in 6f Chelmsford maiden later in October; this Mohaather filly is worth another chance.
runners.comment: Debut Goodwood second but only eighth at Chelmsford after; worth another chance

runners.name: Uncertainty
runners.spotlight: Showed fair form at 6f/7f without winning for Richard Hannon at 2yrs but she came in last of four for her new yard in 1m Southwell nursery in November; lots more is needed.
runners.comment: Fair maiden for Richard Hannon but last of four for new yard at Southwell; more is needed

races.race_name: Napoleons Casino Bradford Handicap

runners.name: Daizen
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two nurseries (6f, soft/AW) prior to getting off the mark at the sixth attempt in a maiden at Ayr (6f, soft) last September; that was an improved performance but he needs another step forward on this reappearance, in view of the handicapper's reaction.
runners.comment: Improved form when winning Ayr maiden last September; needs another step forward on return

runners.name: Reciprocated
runners.spotlight: Looked seriously good when winning last June on his first two starts (6f, good/soft); failed to kick on subsequently in Listed race, Group 2 and a nursery, but returns having been gelded and he's a strong contender if tapping back into his initial promise; wears first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Failed to kick on last season but looked seriously good to begin with; has been gelded
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Daydreama
runners.spotlight: Won two novice contests last summer (good; including C&D) and kept on for close third of 14 in sales race at York (7f, good) in October; his peak form puts him in the picture but there's a suspicion the drop back in trip will be against him on his reappearance; gelded since last time.
runners.comment: Close third in sales race at York last October but that was over 7f and he got outpaced

runners.name: Alaminos
runners.spotlight: Won a big-field maiden on debut at Thirsk (6f, good to firm) last August; disappointing at Haydock (6f, good) in September but pulled hard; brings potential to this 3yo campaign and could prove to be ahead of the handicapper, provided he settles better.
runners.comment: Won on debut last August; disappointing next time but pulled hard; retains potential
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Cape Ashizuri
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Ayr (6f, good to soft) last July; finished last in Group 2 next time and runner-up in Redcar novice (6f, good) last November when last seen; gelded since; not obviously well treated off opening mark but his pedigree provides hope that he'll have more to offer.
runners.comment: Plenty of promise last year; not obviously well treated but he could have more to offer

runners.name: Awraad
runners.spotlight: Won on debut here (5f, good) last April and close second to Anaisa in Listed race at Ripon in August (6f, good; albeit weak form for the grade); disappointing in nursery at Carlisle shortly after and she's 9lb higher for this return to action, but she's not ruled out for a trainer among the winners.
runners.comment: Course novice winner who was Listed runner-up last August; not ruled out on reappearance

runners.name: Anaisa
runners.spotlight: She ran 11 times last year and won twice, including Listed race at Ripon (6f, good) last August; that was relatively modest form for the grade and she needs to prove she's capable off this sort of mark, but is not discounted.
runners.comment: Listed win at Ripon last August; that form isn't particularly strong but not discounted

races.race_name: RIU Hotels And Resorts Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Marhaba Ghaiyyath
runners.spotlight: Progressive in last year's 3yo campaign, going close at Glorious Goodwood (1m2f, good) in July; did pretty well to finish sixth on last month's return at Kempton (1m3f, AW) where he failed to settle and suffered interference; that run may have got the freshness out of him; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Progressive in last year's 3yo campaign and on the shortlist after an encouraging comeback

runners.name: Have Secret
runners.spotlight: Won at Redcar (1m2f, good) last November and not beaten far when fifth of 12 at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) last month; could show the benefit of that latest outing today and he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Won at Redcar last November and could build on last month's fifth at Doncaster

runners.name: Parlando
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins in December (1m2f/9.4f) and good third at Kempton (1m4f, AW) last month; no threat at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) last time but that was a hot contest and this three-time turf winner is not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: Plenty of good AW form since December; three wins on turf; not ruled out each-way

runners.name: Sunriseontheboyne
runners.spotlight: Four wins in Ireland last year and he's one to watch in the betting, but he's finished down the field at big odds on his three outings in Britain this year and is 2lb out of the handicap today.
runners.comment: Four wins in Ireland last year but hasn't shown in Britain this year and is 2lb wrong here
runners.owner: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam

runners.name: Antrim
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) in December and Meydan (1m3f, dirt) in January; merely mid-division back in Britain at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) recently but that was a hotter race than this and he's not written off.
runners.comment: Two wins this winter; merely mid-division at Newcastle recently but not written off

runners.name: Insanity
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on final run last term but on heavy ground, and it was a campaign in which he frequently ran really well, going close off this mark at York (1m2f, good) in October; won on his reappearance in 2024; respected.
runners.comment: Went close off this mark at York last October; won on 2024 reappearance; respected

runners.name: Military Air
runners.spotlight: Useful in France, finishing third in a Listed race last season (1m4f, good to soft); changed hands for 45,000gns in October and his new trainer has done extremely well with French recruits over the years; interesting to see how he goes in the betting on stable debut, for which he wears a hood.
runners.comment: Trainer has done extremely well with French recruits over the years; betting interesting

runners.name: Fireblade
runners.spotlight: Did well for his new yard last season, winning twice (1m4f/2m, good to firm); his current mark maybe isn't beyond him but he's never run over a trip this short and he's probably vulnerable on his reappearance.
runners.comment: 2 wins last year (1m4f/2m) but probably vulnerable over a trip this short on reappearance
runners.owner: Elb Bloodstock Ltd And R L Banks

runners.name: Romieu
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced, ex-French 5yo; up and down among his five British starts last season but ran some big races, including first time up, and he should be capable of winning off his current mark.
runners.comment: Up and down in Britain last season but should be capable of winning off his current mark

runners.name: Savvy Victory
runners.spotlight: His form deteriorated last year and he went 0-9; however, he's dropped down the weights and could improve for his recent comeback in a Listed race at Kempton (fourth); this C&D winner is not ruled out.
runners.comment: Went 0-9 last year but he's dropped down the weights and could improve for recent comeback

runners.name: Flying Frontier
runners.spotlight: Ran just three times last year, winning at Sandown (1m2f, good to firm) on the second occasion, and soft ground there may not have suited when last seen; also unproven on good to soft; this lightly raced 6yo is capable of having a big say if fully tuned up and handling conditions.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 6yo; two wins from last four starts; could have a big say if fully tuned up

races.race_name: Bowel Cancer Screening Programme Save Lives Handicap

runners.name: Yorkshire Glory
runners.spotlight: Gained a superb ninth success of the winter in 6f Newcastle handicap 25 days ago; gamely making all; reverts to turf now off a career-high mark but he's a big player once more.
runners.comment: Gained superb ninth success of winter at Newcastle latest; player once more back on turf

runners.name: Valentine Catcher
runners.spotlight: Didn't prove the easiest to catch right in 2025 and only ninth of 15 in 6f Thirsk handicap 11 days ago; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Didn't prove easy to catch right in 2025; ninth of 15 in 6f Thirsk handicap 11 days ago

runners.name: Azure Zain
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 largely in good form but he came in last of 11 on his return at Southwell (6f) in February, hanging left; handily weighted if back on his A-game here though.
runners.comment: Last of 11 on return at Southwell in February; handily weighted if back on his A-game here

runners.name: Diamont Katie
runners.spotlight: A fair 6f scorer in the first half of 2025; made an encouraging return from seven months off on the AW this winter until only fifth of seven at Southwell (6f; tongue tied) last month; has since undergone breathing surgery and no surprise to see her bounce back.
runners.comment: Encouraging AW return until Southwell fifth last month; had wind op and could bounce back
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Lxxiv & M Hulin

runners.name: Wish This
runners.spotlight: Signed off last season with 6f success at Newcastle and he wasn't disgraced after five months off when sixth in 6f Southwell handicap ten days ago, fading late on; possibilities.
runners.comment: Not disgraced after five months off with Southwell sixth ten days ago; possibilities

runners.name: Hectic
runners.spotlight: Scored at Ripon (6f) in a relatively light campaign in 2025 and back this year with solid fourth placings in handicaps at Newcastle (7f) and Chelmsford (6f); ought to be in the shake-up eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Back this year with solid fourths at Newcastle and Chelmsford; ought to be in the shake-up

runners.name: Palmarian
runners.spotlight: Capable of fair form but he failed to go on for John and Thady Gosden last season; off five months before a patiently-ridden seventh of ten for his new yard in 6f Southwell handicap 19 days ago; can build on it now.
runners.comment: Failed to go on last season; patiently-ridden seventh for new yard at Southwell on return
runners.owner: MDP Racing And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: No Return
runners.spotlight: A fair ex-Irish 6f scorer in 2025; kickstarted this year with a good Lingfield second but below par since, hood tried when beating one back there 25 days ago; more is needed back on turf.
runners.comment: Good Lingfield second at start of year but below par since, beating one there latest

runners.name: Hi Lord
runners.spotlight: A fair 5f winner for Roger Fell in 2024 but has offered little promise for his current yard, visored when beating one at Southwell (6f) last month.
runners.comment: A fair 5f winner for Roger Fell in 2024 but has offered little promise for current yard

runners.name: Great
runners.spotlight: A fair 6f winner for Richard Hannon in 2025; changed hands for 25,000 guineas and not discredited on just his second run for new yard when sixth of 15 in 6f Thirsk handicap 11 days ago; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Not discredited for new yard when sixth at Thirsk 11 days ago; enters calculations

runners.name: Newsreader
runners.spotlight: A three-time 7f winner for Kevin Philippart de Foy in 2024; largely in good form for his current yard last term so he's no forlorn hope on his return.
runners.comment: Largely in good form for his current yard last term so he's no forlorn hope on his return

races.race_name: Into The Sky Pontefract Marathon Handicap

runners.name: Edwardtheninth
runners.spotlight: 0-11 but he's run some good races at up to an extended 2m on AW since joining this yard and he's dropped some hints that he could be suited by this extreme test of stamina.
runners.comment: 0-11 but some good runs on AW for this yard and could be suited by extreme test of stamina

runners.name: Royal Deeside
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on his last three Flat starts but ran well when second here (2m2f, good) last April and he's been in good form over fences this month; could be a contender.
runners.comment: In good form over fences this month and he ran well here last April; could be a contender

runners.name: Lillistar
runners.spotlight: Below her best over hurdles 16 days ago but runner-up on the Flat at Newcastle (2m, AW) last month; consistent in this code last August-October and she's shaped as though this test of stamina could suit; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Below best over hurdles recently but good run on AW previously and this test could suit

runners.name: Charlie Darling
runners.spotlight: Ran well at up to an extended 2m2f on turf in Ireland for Gavin Cromwell and he returned to form when third at Southwell (2m, AW) recently on his third start back from a long absence; in with a chance.
runners.comment: Back to form when third on AW recently and he stayed well in Ireland; in with a chance

runners.name: Izakaya
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row on the Flat (2m1f/2m, soft) last autumn and running well when falling three out on hurdle debut in December; always in rear back on the Flat last month but that came after a 109-day break and she could leave than run behind this afternoon.
runners.comment: Two wins last autumn and she could improve for last month's return to action on AW

runners.name: Lady Phoebe
runners.spotlight: Just one win from her 28 starts and she needs to prove her stamina, but she's on a competitive mark on her Flat form last August/September and she ran pretty well over hurdles three weeks ago; trainer won this last year.
runners.comment: On a competitive mark and ran pretty well over hurdles latest; trainer won this last year

runners.name: Lion City
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who is lightly raced on the Flat but he's failed to threaten in his two handicaps in this code and has to raise his game.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; lightly raced on the Flat but he's failed to threaten in his two handicaps

runners.name: Home And Dry
runners.spotlight: Best Flat form has come on AW and he was tailed off over hurdles 19 days ago; however, that was his first run since last October and back then he won in this code at Chelmsford (2m, AW); has shown some promise on grass over hurdles; not discounted.
runners.comment: Tailed off over hurdles recently but after some time off; won his last Flat outing

runners.name: Achnamara
runners.spotlight: Well beaten over hurdles on his last two starts but hasn't run badly on his last two Flat outings (1m4f/2m, AW) and his trainer has won two of the last six runnings of this race; not discounted.
runners.comment: Hasn't run badly on last two Flat starts and trainer has won two of the last six runnings

runners.name: Different Drum
runners.spotlight: Out of form on the Flat between November-January but ran well here last June and he won over hurdles at Carlisle 17 days ago; could be firmly involved now he's back on song.
runners.comment: Won over h'dles at Carlisle recently and ran well here last June; could be firmly involved

runners.name: Capone
runners.spotlight: He's tumbled down the weights but this 11yo has struggled on AW on his three starts this year and a major turnaround is necessary.
runners.comment: 11yo who has struggled on AW on his three starts this year; major turnaround is necessary
runners.owner: Maximum Racing And D Ffrench Davis

runners.name: Ebendi
runners.spotlight: He's dropped to a basement mark but his sole win came in 2020 and this 9yo struggled to get competitive last year; first run since last October.
runners.comment: Basement mark but sole win came in 2020 & this 9yo struggled to get competitive last year

races.race_name: Constant Security Serving Yorkshire Racecourses Handicap

runners.name: Filly's Last Lady
runners.spotlight: A two-time 5f/6f winner in 2025 for Keiran Burke but yet to fire in two runs for her current yard and had been off five months before seventh of 12 in 6f Catterick handicap 13 days ago; needs to build on that.
runners.comment: Yet to fire in two runs for this yard; Catterick seventh latest; needs to build on that

runners.name: Betweenthesticks
runners.spotlight: Returned from six months off with success in 5f Wolverhampton handicap 17 days ago; up 2lb but this C&D scorer is not taken lightly in his current mood.
runners.comment: Returned with Wolverhampton win; this C&D scorer is not taken lightly in his current mood
runners.owner: J A Swinburne & Mrs Ruth A Carr

runners.name: Dark Kestrel
runners.spotlight: Kept busy on the AW this winter and already a dual 5f winner at Newcastle; not out of things if translating that form but he's yet to show his best form on turf.
runners.comment: A dual 5f winner at Newcastle this year but yet to show his best form on turf

runners.name: Hidden Verse
runners.spotlight: A two-time 5f scorer in 2025; not disgraced after eight months off when sixth of 13 in 5f Bath handicap 16 days ago; one to consider with that run under his belt.
runners.comment: Not disgraced after eight months off when sixth at Bath recently; one to consider

runners.name: Tanjen
runners.spotlight: Scored twice on AW in 2025 last autumn but he wasn't seen out after a poor 12th at Southwell (5f) in September; needs to hit the ground running after seven months off.
runners.comment: Not seen out after poor 12th at Southwell in September; needs to hit the ground running

runners.name: Fuji Mountain
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2024 but he went without his usual tongue tie (still absent) when beating one in 5f Doncaster handicap on his recent return; blinkers go on now and he could take a step forward.
runners.comment: Beat one at Doncaster on return; blinkers go on now and he could take a step forward

runners.name: Komorkis
runners.spotlight: A fair 5f winner for Richard Fahey in 2025; changed hands for 1,400 guineas after but she made an encouraging start for her new yard with fourth of seven in 5f Nottingham handicap 13 days ago; in the mix with hood added.
runners.comment: Encouraging start for new yard with Nottingham fourth 13 days ago; in the mix with hood on

runners.name: Sergeant Mayer
runners.spotlight: Off six months and went without his usual cheekpieces when 11th over C&D two weeks ago; he scored twice at Ripon (5f) last May so no surprise to see him take a step forward here in refitted headgear.
runners.comment: Without cheekpieces when C&D 11th latest; can take a step forward in refitted headgear

runners.name: Midnight Lir
runners.spotlight: Without a victory since 2023 and he beat one in 5f Newcastle handicap when last seen out in October; lots more is needed after six months off.
runners.comment: Without a victory since 2023 and he beat one at Newcastle when last seen out in October

runners.name: Military Girl
runners.spotlight: Scored on her reappearance at Catterick (5f) last season and mainly performed with credit after; she's now 3lb below that winning mark so needs considering.
runners.comment: Scored on Catterick return last season and mainly performed with credit after; considered

runners.name: Speeding Bullet
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark for his current yard over 6f here last June and signed off with an excellent runner-up at Haydock (5f) in September; that form worked out well and he could have more to offer in 2026 so he's a player.
runners.comment: Good Haydock second in September; course winner could have more to offer in 2026; player
runners.owner: Slaters Arms Racing And Ged Shields

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Priscilla's Hope
runners.spotlight: February foal; Ubettabelieveit filly; fifth foal; dam 6f winner (RPR 79), sister to French 7.5f/1m1f winner Lagoa, half-sister to French 1m4f winner Luminosite, out of unraced half-sister to US 1m Grade 2 winner Tam Lin; watch the betting on debut.
runners.comment: February foal; Ubettabelieveit filly; fifth foal; dam 6f winner (RPR 79); watch betting

runners.name: Viking Barbie
runners.spotlight: March foal; 78,000euros yearling; Starman filly; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Alpine Girl (5f/6f; RPR 76) and Beano Power (5f; 65); dam unraced half-sister to 1m2f Listed winners Splashdown (dam of 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Aktabantay) and Cosmodrome; market support should be noted on debut.
runners.comment: March foal; 78,000euros yearling; half-sister to Alpine Girl and Beano Power; one to note

runners.name: Hamdani Mokhater
runners.spotlight: March foal; £25,000 yearling; Mohaather colt; tenth foal; half-brother to six winners including Al Messila (1m; RPR 87), Muatadel (5f-7.4f; 86), Born Slippy (6f 2yo; 83), Cashla Bay (7f 2yo; 82) and Fard (6f; 72); dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo/Group 3; 109), out of 5f winner; dam won on her debut and he's one to note in the betting.
runners.comment: March foal; £25,000 yearling; half-brother to six winners; dam a 6f Listed/Group 3 winner

runners.name: O'Gorman
runners.spotlight: February foal; 20,000gns yearling; Sergei Prokofiev colt; fourth foal; dam unraced half-sister to 7f-1m winner Diamondonthehill, out of 7.5f/1m winning half-sister to winners Summer Chorus (5f/6f), Pirate's Song (6f) and Yuri Gagarin (1m/1m3f); watch the betting on debut.
runners.comment: February foal; 20,000gns yearling; dam unraced half-sister to Diamondonthehill

runners.name: Siouxperb
runners.spotlight: April foal; 90,000gns foal, 110,000gns yearling; Sioux Nation colt; fifth foal; brother to Group-placed 6f/7f winner Ancestral Land (including 2yo; RPR 103), half-brother to 5f/6f winner Staincliff (including 2yo; 99); dam 5f 2yo winner (76), half-sister to Listed-placed 5f winner Online Alexander; speedy pedigree and he's of interest on debut.
runners.comment: April foal; 90,000gns foal, 110,000gns yearling; speedy pedigree and of interest on debut

runners.name: Taseem
runners.spotlight: March foal; 1,500gns yearling; Tasleet colt; fourth foal; dam placed 7f/1m in France, half-sister to US 1m1f Grade 2 winner Proudinsky and German 1m Group 3 winner Proudance; best watched on debut unless the market speaks favourably.
runners.comment: March foal; 1,500gns yearling; dam placed 7f/1m in France; best watched on his debut

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Brilliant Star
runners.spotlight: Showed signs of inexperience but shaped well when second of seven on her debut at Newcastle (1m, AW; 11-4) in November; switches to turf having been off since and should appreciate this step up in trip (out of an Irish Oaks winner); leading contender.
runners.comment: Shaped well when runner-up over 1m at Newcastle on debut; should appreciate this trip

runners.name: Clarissa Eclipse
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; half-sister to winners Colonia (8.5f/9.5f including US Grade 3; RPR 97) and Edestriav (French 11.5f); dam unraced half-sister to 1m Group 2 winner Don Bosco; market should guide on debut.
runners.comment: Half-sister to winners Colonia (US Grade 3) and Edestriav; market should guide on debut

runners.name: Kenkelly
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns yearling; tenth foal; sister to 1m2f/11.6f winner Handsome (RPR 83), half-sister to four winners including Ranch Hand (1m3f-2m including Listed; 109) and Victoria Pollard (1m2f; 88); dam 1m6f winner (101), half-sister to 1m Group 2 winner Passing Glance; interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: 40,000gns yearling; sister to Handsome and half-sister to four winners; dam 1m6f winner

runners.name: Fanciulla Del West
runners.spotlight: Shaped with encouragement on her debut at Newcastle (1m, AW) in November and she built on that when third of seven over that same C&D next time; off since December but her pedigree suggests she'll be suited by this longer trip.
runners.comment: Showed ability in both starts over 1m at Newcastle; pedigree suggests this trip will suit

runners.name: Millie's A Minx
runners.spotlight: Beaten 39l and 48l, the latter here over 1m, in two starts last July; absent since and the step up in trip needs to bring about major improvement; best watched.
runners.comment: Beaten 39l and 48l in two starts last July (7f/1m); absent since and steps up in trip

races.race_name: Get Raceday Ready Handicap

runners.name: Pantile's Gift
runners.spotlight: Built on the encouragement of his handicap debut and pulled 4.5l clear to win at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) eight days ago; unexposed over this trip and holds solid claims back on turf under a 6lb penalty.
runners.comment: Got off the mark when pulling 4.5l clear at Newcastle recently; 6lb penalty; solid claims

runners.name: Crimson Rambler
runners.spotlight: Has form figures of 2221142 in handicaps this year since the blinkers went on, with the wins coming at Southwell (1m, AW) and Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW); has gone up 3lb for his recent runner-up effort at Nottingham but he should go well if seeing out this longer trip.
runners.comment: Has form figures of 2221142 in handicaps this year; should go well if seeing out the trip

runners.name: King Of Berkshire
runners.spotlight: Sent off favourite when third of six here over 7f on his debut last year and he was well beaten at York (1m, good) next time; 16l fourth on his return at Southwell (1m, AW) 26 days ago but he could be suited by this longer trip on handicap debut.
runners.comment: 16l fourth on his return at Southwell (1m) last month; could be suited by this longer trip

runners.name: Room Fourteen
runners.spotlight: Posted an improved effort on her third start when third in a maiden at Lingfield (1m, AW) in December and she filled that same position next time in a novice at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW; second has won since) last month; raced a tad freely on that occasion and a hood is now added for this turf/handicap debut; claims.
runners.comment: Raced a tad freely when third at Wolverhampton last month; hood added; turf/h'cap debut

runners.name: Izz'nt She Hot
runners.spotlight: Hasn't progressed since she was short-headed at Southwell (7f, AW) in September and she beat only one rival home when fifth in a handicap at Kempton (1m, AW) 22 days ago; since been dropped 3lb and now steps up to 1m2f.
runners.comment: Short-headed over 1m at Southwell in September but hasn't progressed since; up in trip

runners.name: Joyful Lawyer
runners.spotlight: Well held in two starts in November/December; gelded and first-time visor when showing more at Chelmsford (1m2f, AW) last month; failed to build on that at Southwell (1m4f, AW) 16 days ago and now drops back in trip with blinkers tried on his turf/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Showed more at Chelmsford last month but didn't build on that last time; turf/h'cap debut

runners.name: Kokushoku
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in small fields (7f/1m) on her first two starts earlier this year but he's failed to match that level in two starts since, including on his handicap debut at Nottingham (8.5f, good; first-time visor) 13 days ago; work to do to reverse that form with Crimson Rambler.
runners.comment: Behind Crimson Rambler when sixth on his handicap debut last time; visor is retained

runners.name: Heated Moment
runners.spotlight: Fifth in a Newbury (7f) maiden on her debut last August but she didn't progress from that in her next four starts (6f/7f), including two handicaps; has left Ollie Sangster since last seen and now steps up in trip for her yard debut having been bought for 4,000gns.
runners.comment: Failed to progress from her debut for Ollie Sangster; up in trip for this yard debut

runners.name: Boilermaker
runners.spotlight: Seventh on his debut at Salisbury (1m, heavy) and twice on the AW (also over 1m) in novice events last year; gelded since last seen 141 days ago and now steps up in trip for his handicap debut; market should be informative.
runners.comment: Seventh in three novice events over 1m last year; gelded; steps up in trip for h'cap debut
runners.owner: W G & A G Vestey, E Gerber And P Inglett

races.race_name: Infobond Technical Manpower Services Handicap

runners.name: Fenlander
runners.spotlight: Remains winless but he resumed from four months off with a good second of nine in 6f Southwell handicap 16 days ago; in the mix nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: Good second of nine in 6f Southwell handicap 16 days ago; in the mix nudged up 1lb

runners.name: My Mate Kev
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Newmarket (6f) last May but well below that form subsequently, though not best drawn when 12th at Doncaster (5f) on his return; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Resumed winning ways at Newmarket (6f) last May but well below that form subsequently

runners.name: Caragio
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful 7f winner at his best; gambled on for his new yard and and shaped well when second of 11 in 7f Chelmsford handicap 26 days ago, just edged out having been set plenty to do; up 3lb but he can make amends with cheekpieces refitted.
runners.comment: Shaped well for new yard with Chelmsford second latest, set lot to do; can go one better

runners.name: Bill Plumb
runners.spotlight: A dual C&D scorer last autumn; yet to hit top form this term but he wasn't best drawn when eighth of 12 in 6f Southwell handicap in February; possibilities from an easing mark after a break.
runners.comment: Dual C&D scorer; not best drawn when eighth at Southwell in February; possibilities

runners.name: Ironist
runners.spotlight: Consistent sort who got off the mark over C&D in September and backed it up with a solid fourth back over C&D following month; she can't be ruled out on her return.
runners.comment: C&D winner; solid fourth back over C&D final run; she can't be ruled out on her return

runners.name: Dark Side Thunder
runners.spotlight: Scored at Chelmsford (6f) in December and not so bad as his recent form figures suggests, challenging wide when ninth in 6f Southwell handicap last month; this C&D winner is not out of things.
runners.comment: Challenged wide when ninth at Southwell last month; this C&D winner is not out of things

races.race_name: Seadell Shops & Chalets At Hemsby Handicap

runners.name: Argy Bhaji
runners.spotlight: Third in two starts on the AW in October and ran another solid race when second of 11 at Newcastle on his 6f reappearance (having been gelded) 33 days ago; switches to turf for his handicap debut and looks a contender.
runners.comment: Third in two starts last year and runner-up at Newcastle on his return; h'cap/turf debut

runners.name: Eightthreeone
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in maiden company last year and made a winning handicap debut on his reappearance at Southwell (6f, AW) two weeks ago; returning to turf should be no problem and he's a contender following a 2lb rise.
runners.comment: Made a winning handicap debut at Southwell two weeks ago; contender following a 2lb rise

runners.name: Eyes Front
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in maiden company on two occasions last June (both 7f) but he's not built on those efforts in three handicaps, including two since being gelded; drops back to 6f with cheekpieces back on (worn once previously); others preferred.
runners.comment: Twice runner-up in maiden company last year (7f); has failed to progress in handicaps

runners.name: Space Bear
runners.spotlight: Won over 7f at Wolverhampton last July when trained by Marco Botti; good second at Lingfield (6f, AW) on stable debut and ran another solid race over that C&D when fifth 18 days ago; claims if coping with the return to turf.
runners.comment: Won over 7f on his debut last July; solid efforts over 6f in two starts for this stable

runners.name: Love Alive
runners.spotlight: Finished off well to win by a neck at Wolverhampton (5f) in February but she was only fifth next time and failed to beat a rival home 39 days ago; steps back up in trip needing a return to form.
runners.comment: Won by a neck at Wolverhampton (5f) in February but failed to beat a rival last time

runners.name: Wild Rosie
runners.spotlight: Shaped with promise when fourth on her debut at Southwell (1m, good to firm) last August but she was well held in her next two starts (both at around 1m); has since left Owen Burrows and drops to 6f for her handicap/yard debut (same owner).
runners.comment: Held in both starts since a promise debut (all at around 1m); drops to 6f for yard debut

runners.name: Angel Summer
runners.spotlight: Well held in two starts last year but she showed more when 4l third of five in a novice at Lingfield (6f, AW) 87 days ago; now makes her turf and handicap debut; open to improvement.
runners.comment: Showed more when a 4l third at Lingfield in January; makes her turf and handicap debut

runners.name: Ocean Of Storms
runners.spotlight: Stepped forward from his first two efforts when fourth of nine at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in October; subsequently gelded but he pulled too hard on handicap debut at Newcastle (7f, AW; hooded) 61 days ago; drops back in trip with the hood removed.
runners.comment: Pulled too hard on handicap debut over 7f two months ago; drops back to 6f; hood removed

races.race_name: AKS Skip Hire Services Handicap

runners.name: Philanthropist
runners.spotlight: Both of his wins have been on AW but he does act on turf; strong in the betting when seventh at Newcastle (7f, AW) on his reappearance 25 days ago, albeit beaten only 2l; claims with cheekpieces first time.
runners.comment: Both wins on the AW but does act on turf; seventh on his return; cheekpieces now go on

runners.name: Rogue Allegiance
runners.spotlight: Sole win came on his debut at Newcastle (6f, AW) in December 2024 but he ran well when runner-up at Leicester (6f, good) on his stable debut eight days ago, shaping as if he's worth another go at 7f; contender if backing that effort up.
runners.comment: Without a win since his debut but he ran well on his first start for this yard; up in trip

runners.name: Miletus
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 13 starts came in a 1m novice at Windsor on soft ground in May 2024 when trained by Charlie Hills; runner-up at Lingfield (1m, AW) on stable debut in February and again at Doncaster latest (7f, good to soft; first-time tongue-tie); hood is now added; quicker ground would be a concern.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two of his three starts for this stable; hood goes on; ground is a concern

runners.name: Grabajabba
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner on the AW over 7f and he ran well despite not really settling when second of 16 at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) 23 days ago; that was just his third go on the grass and he can remain competitive.
runners.comment: Lightly raced on grass and he ran well when runner-up at Doncaster 23 days ago; claims

runners.name: Shimmering Spin
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 15 starts came in a maiden at Chelmsford (1m, AW) in September but he's mostly struggled since and failed to beat a rival home at Southwell (1m, AW) last time on his first start for 112 days; now drops to 7f with cheekpieces applied on this return to turf.
runners.comment: Mostly struggled since winning a maiden at Chelmsford; drops to 7f with cheekpieces added

runners.name: Captain Parma
runners.spotlight: Doubled his tally on the AW when winning at Chelmsford (7f; first-time visor) in February and he was a close third there next time; however, below best in both starts on turf, including at Doncaster (7f, good to soft) 23 days ago; headgear last five starts but not today.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner but he's been below his best in both starts on turf, including last time

runners.name: Man Of Desert
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but his best efforts have been at this track over 1m, including when fourth of seven in a handicap on good ground last June; dropped away tamely at Newcastle (1m, AW) in October on his first start after being gelded and now tries 7f for the first time having been off since.
runners.comment: 0-7 but his best efforts have been here over 1m; struggled when last seen; now tries 7f

runners.name: Mister Mojito
runners.spotlight: 2-2 over C&D, with the most recent of those wins coming last May off the same mark as today; solid efforts on the AW when last seen and he's since undergone wind surgery for the second time; contender back at this track.
runners.comment: 2-2 over C&D and posted some solid efforts when last seen; has since had wind surgery

runners.name: Candonomore
runners.spotlight: Won four times for Tim Easterby (7f/1m), the most recent off 4lb lower at Newcastle (7f, AW) in September; runner-up at Kempton (1m, AW) last time on his fourth start for this yard and he won't mind the return to 7f back on turf; place claims.
runners.comment: Four-time winner for previous yard; runner-up over 1m at Kempton latest start; claims

runners.name: Gorgeous Mr George
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner (two over C&D), the most recent coming last August off 13lb higher, and he was runner-up here over 1m in October off today's mark; below his best when last seen 182 days ago but he could bounce back on his return.
runners.comment: Three-time course winner; below his best when last seen 182 days ago but could bounce back

runners.name: Dashing Dick
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in 2021; posted some good efforts last year, including here (form figures 3213432235 at this track), and ended with a win at Newmarket (6f, good to soft) in November; yet to win from a mark this high and he's usually needed his reappearance.
runners.comment: Has posted some good efforts at this track; 4lb higher than when last seen and may need it

runners.name: Documenting
runners.spotlight: 13yo who has won on 12 occasions, the latest in December 2024 at Wolverhampton; on a good mark but he ended last season with some below-par efforts and is likely best watched on his first start for 194 days.
runners.comment: 13yo who has won on 12 occasions; on a good mark but likely best watched on his return
runners.owner: Kevin Frost Racing Club & Mike Humphries

races.race_name: Mark Sumner Golf Supplies Handicap

runners.name: She's Crafty
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden but she posted an encouraging effort when fourth of 17 at Leicester (7f, good) on her stable debut eight days ago, doing best of those from off the pace and leaving the impression she's worth another go at 1m; holds claims.
runners.comment: 0-9 but did best of those from off the pace at Leicester on stable debut; back up to 1m

runners.name: Akrivos
runners.spotlight: 0-10 but he has posted some solid efforts on the AW; below his best at Southwell (7f, AW) 26 days ago and the switch back to turf isn't sure to suit; cheekpieces now added.
runners.comment: 0-10 but has posted some solid efforts on the AW; below his best latest; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Lucky Sevens
runners.spotlight: Didn't beat a rival home on his first three starts and he dropped away up the straight on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) 36 days ago, having been caught wide early on; cheekpieces added for his turf debut.
runners.comment: Dropped away up straight last time having been wide early; cheekpieces for this turf debut

runners.name: Very Demure
runners.spotlight: Well held in each of her five starts, including in handicap company on her last two outings (1m2f/1m4f); now has cheekpieces on for the first time on her AW debut and she may be happier back at 1m.
runners.comment: Well held in each of her five starts but she may be happier back at 1m; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Lady Of Clover
runners.spotlight: Didn't seem to get home at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) on her handicap debut last month but she kept on for sixth under more patient tactics at Southwell (1m, AW) 12 days ago; place claims if handling the return to turf.
runners.comment: Kept on from off the pace for sixth at Southwell last time; claims if handling turf

runners.name: Regal And Real
runners.spotlight: Beat only one rival home on his handicap debut last month and he dropped away up the straight after making a big move at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW; first-time visor) 28 days ago; makes his turf debut.
runners.comment: Dropped away after making a big move at Wolverhampton last time; makes his turf debut

runners.name: Poetic Grace
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in a Newmarket sales race on her debut in October and down the field in two novice events at Lingfield over 7f (sent off favourite on her second start); improvement needed now stepped up in trip with blinkers applied for her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Well beaten in her first three starts over 7f; improvement needed on her handicap debut

runners.name: Three Builders
runners.spotlight: Beat only one rival home in three novice/maiden events last year and that came in a four-runner race; however, he's been gelded since last seen and now has the hood applied for his handicap debut; market support should be noted.
runners.comment: Beat only one rival home in three starts last year; gelded; hood applied for h'cap debut

runners.name: Ibiza Lights
runners.spotlight: Down the field in three starts on the AW at the end of last year (1m/7f); steps back up to 1m for turf debut and she's open to improvement now switched to handicaps; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Down the field in three starts on the AW last year; open to progress on turf/h'cap debut

races.race_name: Race And Rest - Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Blackberry Bold
runners.spotlight: Showed plenty of speed only to be run out of it close home when a place behind Final Appeal on her debut at Nottingham (5f, good) 13 days ago; clearly has ability but similar forcing tactics could leave her vulnerable on this AW debut.
runners.comment: A place behind Final Appeal on her debut at Nottingham 13 days ago.
runners.owner: Upton House Stud & Whitsbury Manor Stud

runners.name: Final Appeal
runners.spotlight: Showed plenty when just edged out by the winner at Nottingham (5f, good; 5-4) 13 days ago on his first outing; this AW debut looks a perfect opportunity to open his account.
runners.comment: Showed plenty when just edged out by the winner at Nottingham 13 days ago on his debut.

runners.name: Marralyn
runners.spotlight: £30,000 yearling; second foal by Minzaal; dam 1m2f winner (RPR 95), half-sister to winners Jureer (French 1m/1m3f) and Cueros (8.5f-1m4f), out of maiden sister to HK/Singaporean 1m-1m2f Group 1 winner Dunboyne Express; stable going well but bred for middle distances and should do better in time.
runners.comment: Stable going well but bred for middle distances and should do better in time.

runners.name: Noble Raider
runners.spotlight: £42,000 yearling; second foal by Persian Force; half-brother to Italian 1m 2yo winner King Of Fire; dam unplaced 6f-1m 2yo (RPR 43), closely related to 7f/7.5f winner Crackin Dream, out of Italian 7f Group 3 winner; his sire was an early sort who won the Brocklesby and yard had their first juvenile winner of the season here this month so must come into consideration on this debut.
runners.comment: Yard had their first juvenile winner of the season here this month.

runners.name: Magical Life
runners.spotlight: Moderate form in two runs this month; easy to dismiss.
runners.comment: Moderate form in two runs this month; easy to dismiss.

races.race_name: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap

runners.name: Crown Inn To Win
runners.spotlight: Has shown improvement since stepping up in trip, finishing second on his last two runs despite taking a keen hold, the latest over C&D last month; sports a first-time hood and if that helps him to settle then he holds strong claims.
runners.comment: Has shown improvement since stepping up in trip, finishing second on his last two runs.

runners.name: Corduroy
runners.spotlight: 0-6 but showed there was a race in him when third in a similar contest over C&D last month; sold for 16,500gns to join new yard subsequently and should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: 0-6 but showed there was a race in him when third over C&D last month.

runners.name: Forever Perfect
runners.spotlight: 0-5 but didn't shape badly up in trip when third of seven over C&D 15 days ago; a similar performance should see her in the frame.
runners.comment: 0-5 but didn't shape badly up in trip when third of seven over C&D 15 days ago.

runners.name: Itsonlyrockandroll
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on all three starts but makes handicap debut at a low level for top yard; no surprise if she shows significant improvement.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on all three starts but makes handicap debut at a low level for top yard.

runners.name: Steel Fixer
runners.spotlight: Well held in three runs last year; tailed off on handicap debut at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) on return to action last month; steps up in trip on AW debut and needs to show significant improvement.
runners.comment: Tailed off on handicap debut at Doncaster on return to action last month.

runners.name: Moon Is Up
runners.spotlight: Yet to be placed from five starts but was only beaten 4l on her return to action at Kempton (1m4f, AW) last month; has been dropped 3lb and should be competitive.
runners.comment: Yet to be placed from five starts but was only beaten 4l on her return last month.

races.race_name: Get Raceday Ready Handicap

runners.name: Shalaa Asker
runners.spotlight: Rated 53 when winning at Chelmsford (5f) in December but has been the model of consistency since (311522); didn't have the clearest of passages when fast-finishing runner-up over C&D latest; key player despite another slight weights nudge.
runners.comment: Fast-finishing runner-up over C&D latest; key player despite another slight weights nudge.

runners.name: Midnight Call
runners.spotlight: Notched her fifth course win since November, justifying favouritism in style (Class 6; C&D) latest; this career-high mark (up 9lb) demands more, but she has to enter calculations with Joanna Mason another positive jockey booking.
runners.comment: Stylish C&D winner latest; a career-high mark (up 9lb) demands more; enters calculations.

runners.name: Em Four
runners.spotlight: Won twice on the AW this year, the most recent from a 2lb lower mark at Kempton (6f); creditable third on turf at Leicester (6f, good; 3-1 favourite) last week; how he'll cope with dropping back to 5f (0-4) remains to be seen; isn't discounted.
runners.comment: Creditable third at Leicester (6f, good; 3-1 favourite) last week; this drop back to 5f?

runners.name: Binadham
runners.spotlight: Yet to win on AW (0-7) and hasn't won anywhere since scoring on debut at Yarmouth (5f maiden) in April 2024; thereabouts lately, including when no extra close home over C&D (5-1) 32 days ago; newly applied cheekpieces are worth a go.
runners.comment: 1-15; close third over C&D (5-1) 32 days ago; these newly applied cheekpieces worth a go.

runners.name: Phoenix Beach
runners.spotlight: Four C&D wins (from marks in the 60s); excuses when a closing third over C&D on penultimate start; hung right when three places (over 4l) behind Shalaa Asker subsequently; has won for Kieran O'Neill but needs everything to drop his way.
runners.comment: Four C&D wins; fair mark but three places (over 4l) behind Shalaa Asker over C&D latest.

runners.name: Smooth Silesie
runners.spotlight: Won at her beloved Yarmouth (5f, good to soft; from 4lb lower) last October; hasn't been at her best since returning to AW recently; this is a fraction easier and she could strip fitter but others are more appealing.
runners.comment: Won at her beloved Yarmouth (5f, good to soft) last October; low-key on AW recently.

runners.name: Diomed Spirit
runners.spotlight: Won from a similar mark at Lingfield (5f; 8-1) in November; form levels have dipped since finishing third back there in January, failing to beat a single rival the last twice; is 2-4 over C&D, so it's possible that added cheekpieces can rekindle a spark.
runners.comment: Form levels have dipped since January; is 2-4 over C&D; can cheekpieces rekindle a spark?

races.race_name: Advance Price Midweek Racing For £10 Handicap

runners.name: Rubellite
runners.spotlight: 6-38, the bulk of them for Stella Barclay; with Richard Fahey for some of 2025, winning over 1m4f here (3lb lower) last September; runner-up to progressive rival at Southwell (1m6f) latest on debut for this trainer; a possible.
runners.comment: Runner-up to progressive rival at Southwell (1m6f) latest on debut for this trainer.

runners.name: Beaune
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark when a shock winner at Lingfield (1m4f; 40-1) in February but showed that was no fluke when runner-up twice since and went in again over C&D 11 days ago; up 5lb but still on the upgrade.
runners.comment: Got off the mark when a shock winner at Lingfield in February; won again over C&D since.

runners.name: Knight Of Magic
runners.spotlight: Has three wins on AW, including two here (one over C&D); tends to race freely and needs to settle better to fulfil his potential; second over hurdles two weeks ago.
runners.comment: Tends to race freely and needs to settle better to fulfil his potential.

runners.name: Late Claim
runners.spotlight: Made successful handicap debut at Lingfield (1m5f, Polytrack) in December and finished runner-up three times since, including over C&D; wheels have come off lately, however, and cheekpieces worn last time are retained.
runners.comment: Made successful handicap debut at Lingfield in December but below that form recently.

runners.name: Wave Rock
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark when winning at this venue (9.5f) in February off 3lb lower for today's jockey Donagh Murphy; pulled too hard to give her any chance at Newcastle (1m2f) last month and she will need to settle to get this extra distance.
runners.comment: Got off the mark when winning at this venue in February off 3lb lower.

runners.name: Phyllis Burton
runners.spotlight: Hasn't gone on since making an encouraging debut when fourth at Lingfield (1m, AW) last April; steps up in trip and hood worn last time is persevered with.
runners.comment: Hasn't gone on since making an encouraging debut when fourth at Lingfield last April.

runners.name: Three Yorkshiremen
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D last November but 10lb rise has seen him falter in five subsequent runs; mark is gradually coming down but doesn't look ready to strike.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D last November but 10lb rise has seen him falter in five subsequent runs.

runners.name: The Bay Warrior
runners.spotlight: Has seven wins on AW and is now 6lb lower than for the last of those last September; doesn't look ready to take advantage though.
runners.comment: Has seven wins on AW and is now 6lb lower than for the last of those last September.

races.race_name: Get The Inside Track With raceday-ready.com Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Star Marian
runners.spotlight: Returned from a 231-day break, making all to beat an odds-on favourite in a five-runner 5f fillies' maiden here three weeks ago; now rated 6lb higher on her first attempt over 6f (dam won over this trip); probable player in a race lacking strength in depth.
runners.comment: Made all, beating odds-on favourite in a 5f fillies' maiden here latest; probable player.

runners.name: Filly Foden
runners.spotlight: Pacy filly; registered back-to-back wins at Southwell and Lingfield (both 5f) earlier this year; disappointing from higher marks since - held up the last twice; the handicapper is cutting her some slack now and she'd be a threat if able to blaze the trail from her wide draw.
runners.comment: Registered back-to-back wins at Southwell & Lingfield (5f) earlier this year; poor since.

runners.name: Havana Joy
runners.spotlight: In the frame in her first five starts (high 60's RPR's), including when twice finding one stronger here (6f-7f); has failed to beat a single rival the last twice (either side of wind surgery) so she has a bit to prove at present; easing mark.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a single rival the last twice (either side of wind surgery); bit to prove.

runners.name: Crystal Dagger
runners.spotlight: With Ed Walker when beaten a head at Lingfield (7f; 28-1) on New Year's Eve; changed hands in February for 5,000gns and she's yet to shine (0-2) for this trainer; competes from an easing mark, back in trip seeking to make it 11th-time lucky.
runners.comment: Beaten a head at Lingfield on New Year's Eve; no progress (0-2) since joining this trainer

runners.name: Daytona Lady
runners.spotlight: Now rated 8lb lower than when running on well to down Jax Edge over C&D (15-2) in September 2024; fair third on return from a near six-month break at Southwell (25-1) recently; 1lb lower and could be worth considering.
runners.comment: Fair third on return from a near six-month break at Southwell (25-1); worth considering.
runners.owner: Eric Broadwith And Mrs Ruth Carr

runners.name: Tabby
runners.spotlight: Ability in five juvenile starts for Denis Quinn, going closest when beaten a neck at Southwell (6f fillies' maiden; 50-1) in October 2024; returns to action for a new trainer off a 478-day absence; best watched unless the market speaks favourably.
runners.comment: 0-5 for D. Quinn; returns to action for a new trainer off a 478-day absence; best watched.

races.race_name: Download The Racecourse App Raceday Ready Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Faster Bee
runners.spotlight: Came good at the 26th attempt over C&D in February when making all; has ran well twice since over C&D when in the money and a danger to all if he can grab an early lead in returning cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Came good at the 26th attempt over C&D in February when making all; has ran well since.

runners.name: Star Of Atlantis
runners.spotlight: Dual winner but ran poorly in a couple of classifieds this year before staying on well in a similar race here (8.5f) last month; on a decent mark but may do better back over 1m.
runners.comment: Stayed on well in a similar race here last month; drop in trip not certain to suit.

runners.name: Port Hedland
runners.spotlight: Hard to win with and made it 0-13 on the AW when held at Southwell ten days ago; steps up in trip but essentially regressive and is some way down the pecking order.
runners.comment: Hard to win with and made it 0-13 on the AW when held at Southwell ten days ago.

runners.name: Sanditon
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Chelmsford (7f, AW) in February but has failed to build on that since; down to a career-low mark and capable if he can return to form.
runners.comment: Got off the mark at Chelmsford in February but has failed to build on that since.

runners.name: Alyara
runners.spotlight: Just 1-34 on AW and although she fared better when fourth here (6f) last time, she is not a good betting proposition; steps back up to 7f.
runners.comment: Just 1-34 on AW and she fared better when fourth here last time.
runners.owner: Lynn Cullimore Pete Ponting Joe Ponting

runners.name: Thomas Equinas
runners.spotlight: All five wins have came at Chelmsford's Polytrack; on a mounting losing run but didn't fare too badly when fourth of 11 at Southwell (7f) ten days ago.
runners.comment: On a mounting losing run but didn't fare too badly when fourth at Southwell ten days ago.

runners.name: Bungle Bay
runners.spotlight: All eight wins have been at this track (five over C&D) but has been below his best this year and doesn't look ready to strike.
runners.comment: Has been below his best this year and doesn't look ready to strike.

runners.name: Tomarlo
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; sole success came at Dundalk (6f) in November 2024; has finished last on two of his three starts for current trainer; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Has finished last on two of his three starts for current trainer.

runners.name: Diligent Henry
runners.spotlight: Both wins have came over C&D; below his best on his last two starts but now back on his last winning mark; not out of this.
runners.comment: Below his best on his last two starts but now back on his last winning mark.

races.race_name: Download The Racecourse App Raceday Ready Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Top Star
runners.spotlight: Yet to win (0-10); runner-up in two of his last three starts, keeping-on to chase home the revitalised Flicka's Girl over 6f here (Class 6 classified; 14-1) four weeks ago; tackles 7f for the first time, a risk even though his dam was a Listed-placed 7f AW winner.
runners.comment: Yet to win (0-10); runner-up to Flicka's Girl over 6f here latest; 7f is the major unknown

runners.name: Ashford Hill
runners.spotlight: 2-21; said to have "benefited for the break" when returning to spring a 16-1 surprise at Lingfield (7f, Class 6) in January; wasn't far off those same levels when fourth (beaten 3l) back there next time but latest Kempton effort was only a modest one.
runners.comment: "Benefited for break" when springing 16-1 surprise at Lingfield (7f) in Jan; mixed since.

runners.name: Bad Habits
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate (1-27) but has twice been edged out in tight finishes recently, latterly when failing to reel in Gladiadora at Southwell (7f; 9-1); pestered for the lead, reportedly running "too free", when disappointing over C&D 11 days ago; possible.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate (1-27) but has twice been edged out in tight finishes recently; possible.

runners.name: Gundogan
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate (1-30); beaten a neck over 6f at Southwell (Class 6) in February but less productive in three subsequent appearances (6f-7f), although with mitigating circumstances (saddle slipped) latest.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate (1-30); beaten a neck over 6f at Southwell in February; no real joy since

runners.name: Street Dancer
runners.spotlight: Cost 32,000gns as a 3yo but has been beaten 26l, 19.5l, and 21l respectively in three 7f AW novices/maidens; is very lowly rated for handicap debut, competing from 15lb out of the handicap; makes no appeal.
runners.comment: Beaten 26l, 19.5l, and 21l respectively in three 7f AW novices/maidens; makes no appeal.

runners.name: The Cutest
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden; never dangerous sixth over 6f here (beaten over 5l; 80-1) a fortnight ago; regular hood retained tackling a seventh furlong - dam won over 7f-1m - for the first time; competes from 1lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: 12-race maiden; tackles seventh furlong - dam won over 7f-1m - for first time; 1lb 'wrong'

runners.name: So Chic
runners.spotlight: Two of her four wins have come over this C&D, partnered by Jack Doughty when running down Coconut Bay in a Class 6 classified (10-1) last March; lacked the pace to figure when sixth (Gundogan fifth; 13-8 favourite) over this trip at Lingfield latest.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner (incl for this rider); lacked for pace when sixth at Lingfield (7f) latest

runners.name: Big Gypsy King
runners.spotlight: With Paul and Oliver Cole when a comfortable winner at Chelmsford (7f, Class 6; 5-2 favourite) last May; poor showings in five starts - 6f Flat to 2m1f hurdle - for this trainer; no reason to envisage a change of headgear working the oracle.
runners.comment: Poor showings in five starts - 6f Flat to 2m1f hurdle - for this trainer; headgear tweaked

runners.name: Ravenglass
runners.spotlight: Two course wins, including when running on well over this trip (Class 6 classified; 9-4 favourite) last March; now rated 1lb lower than when fourth over C&D (25-1) last October; market should guide on seasonal return.
runners.comment: 1lb lower than when fourth over C&D (25-1) last October; let the market offer a guide.

races.race_name: Wolverhampton Holiday Inn Handicap

runners.name: Eagles Whistle
runners.spotlight: Rattled up a hat-trick for Daragh Bourke (7f-9.5f) earlier in the year; similar level of form since joining this trainer, runner-up over 8.5f here on penultimate start, and perhaps found 1m4f too far at Catterick (6-4 favourite) a fortnight ago; chance.
runners.comment: C&D winner; perhaps found 1m4f too far at Catterick (6-4 fav) a fortnight ago; chance.

runners.name: Distinction
runners.spotlight: Won from higher marks at Beverley (1m2f, soft) and Kempton (1m3f) last summer; took a step back in the right direction at Pontefract (fifth) a fortnight ago, and this looks a suitable next objective from 2lb lower; P J McDonald is back aboard.
runners.comment: Step back in the right direction at Pontefract (fifth); this looks a suitable objective.

runners.name: Nuptown Girl
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden (0-12) but she's been consistent (form figures 26253) since joining Ian Williams; drops back in trip having failed to see out 1m4f at Southwell (third of six, beaten 5l) 24 days ago.
runners.comment: Maiden (0-12); consistent (form figures 26253) since joining Ian Williams; back in trip.

runners.name: Karakula
runners.spotlight: Kept on well when making the breakthrough over C&D (from 1lb lower) last September; less effective since, losing a shoe when only fourth of five at Kempton (1m3f); it's possible that she'll return fresher off a 167-day absence.
runners.comment: C&D winner (from 1lb lower) last September; less effective since; is fresh from a break.

runners.name: Kaaranah
runners.spotlight: Caught the eye on penultimate start; justified market support over C&D subsequently (in newly applied cheekpieces), that his first win since September 2023; a 4lb rise looks fair, so he's entitled to be on the premises assuming the headgear continues to have the desired effect.
runners.comment: Back to winning ways in newly applied cheekpieces over C&D latest; a 4lb rise looks fair.

runners.name: Moonlit Cloud
runners.spotlight: No win since May 2024 (1m2f, Bath) but she has fallen a long way in the weights as a result; bogged down on heavy ground at Nottingham last October; it's difficult to know what to expect returned to AW (0-11) off a six-month absence.
runners.comment: No win since May 2024 (1m2f, Bath); best watched on return from a six-month absence.

races.race_name: South West Rail Solutions Ltd Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Kings Champion
runners.spotlight: Won easily at Ayr (2m4f, soft) in January, his second hurdling start, and was not discredited when second to useful rival at Taunton (2m3f, heavy; front pair well clear) in February; ought to make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Easy winner at Ayr and not discredited when clear second to useful rival last time

runners.name: Uptown Dandy
runners.spotlight: Delivered on earlier promise when winning C&D (heavy) maiden last month, recovering well from slow jump at third-last flight; ran to fairly useful level that day but probably needs to improve again to land this prize.
runners.comment: Fairly useful form when winning C&D maiden last month; probably needs more again today

runners.name: Crystal Island
runners.spotlight: Joined Nicky Henderson after winning Irish point debut just over a year ago and impressed when beating useful rivals on rules debut at Ascot (2m5f, soft) in December; not seen since but remains a very good prospect.
runners.comment: Irish point winner who impressed on rules debut at Ascot in December; good prospect

runners.name: Rathkenny
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in last month's EBF Final at Sandown but looked promising beforehand, most notably when winning Doncaster maiden (2m3f, soft) in January; each-way claims if back on song.
runners.comment: Pulled up in competitive handicap last time but looked promising beforehand; e-w claims

runners.name: Home Made Hero
runners.spotlight: Drew 12l clear to make winning rules debut at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good to soft) in October and also ran well on next two outings, albeit a late faller on first occasion; might have to settle for a minor role in this deep race, though.
runners.comment: Good winner on hurdle debut in October but might have to settle for a minor role here

runners.name: Axel Bleue
runners.spotlight: Point and dual bumper winner in 2025; fair form when runner-up in two 2m7f novice hurdles in October but absent since pulled up on handicap debut in November and faces tough assignment here.
runners.comment: Fair form when second twice in October but absent since pulled up in November; tough task

runners.name: Dylans Moon
runners.spotlight: Made pleasing debut when third of nine in Uttoxeter novice hurdle (2m4f, soft) last month and has obvious scope for improvement but needs to take a good step forward here.
runners.comment: Made pleasing debut when third at Uttoxeter last month but needs a good step forward here

runners.name: Fairye Forth
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who was very respectable third of seven on recent rules debut at Chepstow (2m7f, good to soft); will find much easier opportunities than this in due course, though.
runners.comment: Irish point winner; respectable third on recent rules debut but has a lot to find here

runners.name: Cedar Creek
runners.spotlight: Flat-bred 5yo who won good-ground junior bumper at Aintree in November 2024 and was not disgraced in Cheltenham Listed event a few weeks later; makes hurdle debut after 475-day absence and in a strong race, though.
runners.comment: Promising in bumpers last season but makes hurdle debut after long layoff and in good race

runners.name: Pappariche
runners.spotlight: Won a small-field Irish point last February (heavy ground) but was well-beaten 100-1 shot over C&D on rules debut nine days ago.
runners.comment: Well-beaten 100-1 shot over C&D on rules debut nine days ago

runners.name: Malinaero
runners.spotlight: Showed only minor promise on first two outings (bumper/2m3f hurdle) and cannot be recommended in this company.
runners.comment: Showed only minor promise on first two outings (bumper/2m3f hurdle)

runners.name: Yellow Card
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner with some fairly useful middle-distance form on his CV; unseated rider at second flight on hurdle debut in September, though, and was well beaten at Fontwell the following month.
runners.comment: Dual Flat winner; made disappointing start to hurdling career last year; far too risky

races.race_name: SWRSL - Dan Boulton Novices' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Dessie Haze
runners.spotlight: Beat well-handicapped, odds-on favourite when staying on well to open account at Chepstow (2m7f, good to soft) 19 days ago, his eighth start over hurdles; 6lb higher here but may yet have more to offer.
runners.comment: Stayed on well to beat well-handicapped, odds-on favourite at Chepstow this month

runners.name: Stiletto
runners.spotlight: Did well for new stable in early part of season, winning three times under Sean Bowen (2m2f-3m, good/good to soft); returned from break with low-key run last month but has the champion jockey back on board today.
runners.comment: Three wins in early part of season; probably needed last month's run; Bowen back aboard

runners.name: Getaway King
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 8yo who started off for Fergal O'Brien with three chase wins (2m4f-2m5f) in the autumn; returned with a lesser effort in February and fell when last in action but remains unexposed over 3m; sports first-time cheekpieces for hurdling debut; could have a say.
runners.comment: Won three chases in the autumn; has cheekpieces added for this hurdling debut

runners.name: Glenmuick
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in first three handicap hurdles (2m-2m4f); needs combination of new trip and first-time cheekpieces to enable huge improvement.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in first three handicaps; up in trip with cheekpieces added today

runners.name: Moon Over Clyro
runners.spotlight: Placed in two 2m Worcester maidens towards end of last summer but struggled in his first two handicaps (2m3f/2m2f); hiked up in trip with cheekpieces added today; leap of faith required to support him.
runners.comment: Struggled in first two handicaps (2m3f/2m2f); up in trip with cheekpieces added here

runners.name: Hidden Target
runners.spotlight: Bettered low-key handicap debut when keeping-on third at Chepstow (2m3f, good to soft) last month; 1lb lower here and pedigree suggests this longer trip will suit; high on the list.
runners.comment: Kept on for third at Chepstow (2m3f) last month and can improve again over 3m

runners.name: Eaststone Buck's
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in his three qualifying races (1m7f-2m4f) but fared better when keeping-on second (no match for winner) on handicap debut at Stratford (2m6f, soft) six weeks ago; 2lb wrong here but remains unexposed.
runners.comment: Kept on for second on handicap debut; 2lb wrong here but remains unexposed

runners.name: Air Apparent
runners.spotlight: Kept on for third in Hereford maiden (2m3f, good) last month, his fourth start over hurdles, and has plenty of scope for further improvement in staying handicaps; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Third in 2m3f maiden last month and open to more improvement in staying handicaps
runners.owner: A Cocks, T Johnson & P Downing

runners.name: Kastellion
runners.spotlight: Kept on for third on handicap debut at Southwell (2m4f, soft) in February but was disappointing favourite when weakening fourth at Bangor (2m7f, good) last month and needs a better effort here.
runners.comment: Placed over 2m4f on handicap debut but shade disappointing when upped to 2m7f last month

runners.name: Smile Back
runners.spotlight: Placed in three of her four handicaps but was beaten about 19l when weakening third at Carlisle (3m1f, soft) in February and is not making any progress; hood left off here; stablemate Hidden Target appeals more.
runners.comment: Placed in three of her four handicaps but not making any progress; others appeal more

runners.name: Fanny Moon
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only four hurdle runs but was soundly beaten on last month's handicap debut at Bangor (2m3f, good); goes beyond about 2m5f for first time here.
runners.comment: Unexposed after only four hurdle runs but was soundly beaten on handicap debut (2m3f)

runners.name: Golden Prince
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 5yo who was evidently suited by the hike up in trip when third on handicap debut at Bangor (2m7f, good) three weeks ago; probably still has potential; might be the answer.
runners.comment: Placed when upped to 2m7f for recent handicap debut and probably still has potential

runners.name: Washington Parks
runners.spotlight: 0-6 over hurdles when based in Ireland and absent since two down-the-field handicap runs last April (2m3f/2m4f), but still worth a glance in the betting before this stable debut; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: 0-6 over hurdles in Ireland and absent for a year but worth a market check on stable debut

races.race_name: South West Rail Solutions Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jeu d'Opale
runners.spotlight: Ex-French 8yo who opened British account at sixth attempt, with ready success at Wincanton (2m4f, heavy) in February; 9lb higher here and may not want the ground to dry out too much but further progress is possible.
runners.comment: Ready winner on heavy ground in February, his sixth British start; up 9lb

runners.name: Jaccours
runners.spotlight: 1-5 over hurdles, the win gained in course maiden (2m4f, heavy) in January; flopped when warm favourite for chase/handicap debut the following month but could still have a good future over fences.
runners.comment: Course hurdle winner; flopped on chase/handicap debut but may still have potential

runners.name: Gaelic Rambler
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 6yo who unseated rider on chase/handicap debut in January but has made the frame on all three starts since; form of last month's Wetherby second (1m7f, good; first-time cheekpieces) has recently been franked by the winner, and this fellow ought to be suited by this move back up in trip; contender.
runners.comment: Second to a next-time-out winner last month and still has low mileage; contender

runners.name: Hold Up La Colmine
runners.spotlight: Won by wide-margin on seasonal debut at Windsor (2m4f, good to soft) in November but subsequent hurdle/chase form has been underwhelming; needs new cheekpieces to provoke a response.
runners.comment: Wide-margin winner on seasonal debut but subsequent form underwhelming; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Brulure Noire
runners.spotlight: Three-time chase winner (twice here); has form-figures of PU during light campaign this season but is 2lb lower than when clear second over C&D (soft; also suited by good to soft) a year ago; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Failed to complete both races this season but was clear second over C&D a year ago

runners.name: Calli Black
runners.spotlight: Won readily at Worcester (2m4f, good) last spring and is still lightly raced over fences but her subsequent three runs (latest was over hurdles) were disappointing and she's 2lb wrong on first outing since December.
runners.comment: Won at Worcester last spring but subsequent form was disappointing; back from a break

runners.name: Lermoos Legend
runners.spotlight: Gained five of his nine chase wins at this track (2m/2m3f) but absent since below-par run in October and still on a fairly tough mark.
runners.comment: Runs this track very well but has been absent since October

runners.name: Latenightrumble
runners.spotlight: On a good mark now and was seemingly back in good form when close fourth of 11 at Chepstow (2m7f, soft) in February; tended to jump left and was below that level without cheekpieces at Carlisle (3m) last month but has possibilities if combination of drop back in trip, first-time blinkers and return to a left-handed track has desired effect.
runners.comment: Seemingly back in good form two starts ago; back in trip with new headgear tried here

runners.name: Bowenspark
runners.spotlight: Came good over fences with small-field win at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good to soft) in February but was not in the same form on his latest outing and ran poorly the only previous time he wore cheekpieces; others look safer.
runners.comment: Good winner at Bangor in February but safely held since; cheekpieces given another whirl

races.race_name: SWRSL - Alfie Boulton Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Trust House
runners.spotlight: Rewarded strong market support when winning readily at Market Rasen (2m, good to soft) last month, on his second run after a layoff, and is now 2-8 over hurdles; 8lb higher here but still commands respect.
runners.comment: Won readily at Market Rasen last month, his second run after a layoff; commands respect

runners.name: Thankyouandplease
runners.spotlight: Dual 1m7f winner this season (heavy/good to soft); gave good account when second off today's career-high mark at Ayr (2m4f, soft) on Friday and has a fighting chance here, providing this race doesn't come too soon.
runners.comment: Dual 1m7f winner this season; makes quick reappearance after 2m4f Ayr second on Friday

runners.name: Caelan
