runners.spotlight: Improved to win good-ground Worcester maiden (2m, good to soft) in September and subsequently made the frame in two 2m Huntington handicaps (good/good to soft); returns from four-month break on workable mark but riding arrangements suggest Trust House is the stable's main hope.
runners.comment: Returns from break on workable mark but Sean Bowen rides the stable's other one

runners.name: Aeroplane Blonde
runners.spotlight: Has a tendency to race too freely and is usually held up at the back; struggled to get competitive at Chepstow in February and now 0-6 over hurdles but interesting if judged on some of his form towards the end of last year; not out of this if first-time cheekpieces have positive effect.
runners.comment: Struggled last time and now 0-6 over hurdles but interesting if judged on late-2025 form
runners.owner: Anthony R Hunt & Joe & Sharon Tumilty

runners.name: Asta La Vista
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 5yo who was well beaten in his first two hurdle races but kept on to take a modest third at Wetherby (2m, good) last month and has plenty of scope for further improvement in handicaps.
runners.comment: Kept on for modest third in Wetherby maiden last month; may progress again in handicaps

runners.name: Eastern Shores
runners.spotlight: No match for winner when second on handicap debut at Huntingdon (2m, soft) in February and disappointed when upped to 2m3f at Bangor last month; not yet fully exposed but needs to find something extra here.
runners.comment: Second on handicap debut (2m) but disappointed when upped to 2m3f; more needed here

runners.name: Chillhi
runners.spotlight: Flat and dual hurdle winner during busy summer for James Owen last year but absent since two below-par runs for Alison Thorpe in October; considered only if lively in the market before this stable debut.
runners.comment: Flat and dual hurdle winner last summer but makes stable debut after a layoff

runners.name: Corner Forward
runners.spotlight: Showed only minor promise in his qualifying races (1m7f-2m1f) but may not have handled heavy ground last time; no surprise if he gets much more competitive on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Showed only minor promise in his qualifying races but should improve in handicaps

races.race_name: South West Rail Solutions Ltd Handicap Chase

runners.name: Henry Box Brown
runners.spotlight: Returned from three-month break with respectable fourth here (2m3f, soft) in March last time and will be fine over this longer trip, but he's not really been firing on all cylinders since last summer; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Ran okay here last month, after a break, but others appeal more

runners.name: Esperti
runners.spotlight: In-form 9yo who won four-runner marathon at Fakenham (3m5f, good) in pretty good style last week; also very well suited by this C&D and ground slower than good; respected under 7lb penalty.
runners.comment: Won Fakenham marathon in good style last week and this C&D also suits him well

runners.name: Mythe Bridge
runners.spotlight: Placed in four of his five chases, most recently when third in first-time cheekpieces (retained) at Wetherby (3m, soft) in February; won't mind if the ground dries out and he has another big role to play.
runners.comment: Placed in four of his five chases and has another big role to play

runners.name: Bataillon
runners.spotlight: Unable to justify warm favouritism when well-held fourth at Wincanton (3m1f, good to soft) last month but will appreciate this ease in grade, and the form of his breakthrough win at Taunton (2m7f, good to soft) has worked out well; big player if back on song.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite last time but remains of interest if judged on Taunton win in January

runners.name: Ahanaglogh
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 7yo who came second in his first two chases, the latter at Leicester (2m4f, soft) in February; could have untapped potential over 3m and features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Runner-up in both chases (2m5f/2m4f) and could have untapped potential over 3m
runners.owner: De La Haye Lewis Newman Lennon Appleby

runners.name: Valirann Gold
runners.spotlight: Produced a remarkable late surge to snatch what had looked an unlikely victory at Fontwell (2m5f, good to soft; blinkers removed) last month but this inconsistent 9yo tends to race lazily nowadays and got going too late when back at Fontwell a fortnight ago; other are much less risky.
runners.comment: Produced remarkable late surge to win two starts ago but overall profile is not persuasive

runners.name: Martalmix'jac
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to win from the front at Ludlow (3m, good to soft; front pair clear) in February and remains well handicapped on best of last season's form but he's too inconsistent to rely upon heavily.
runners.comment: Won from the front at Ludlow in February but is too inconsistent to rely upon heavily

runners.name: John W Creasy
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner on slow ground last season (2m/2m3f) but out of sorts this term and has a lot to prove.
runners.comment: Dual chase winner last season but out of sorts this term

runners.name: Tommys Charm
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced for his age and now 12lb lower than when going close at Wincanton (3m1f, good to soft) at start of last year, but he's been out of form this season and needs first-time tongue-tie to aid a revival.
runners.comment: Out of form this season and needs new tongue-tie to aid a revival

runners.name: Swift Jet
runners.spotlight: Fell in his first two chases (both this winter); bounced back with 14l win over hurdles at Doncaster (3m, good to soft) in February but ran poorly when last in action and is very hard to predict on this return to fences.
runners.comment: Fell in first two chases; subsequently won over hurdles but pulled up on latest outing

runners.name: Stadmallen
runners.spotlight: Seen only once under rules since 2021 and this season's pointing form is very uninspiring; 10lb out of the weights for this stable debut; could be considered only if lively in the betting.
runners.comment: Modest pointer; could be considered only if lively in the betting before rules return

runners.name: I Ain't Your Mate
runners.spotlight: Tailed off when upped to 2m5f for chase/handicap debut at Hereford (soft) in January, but the form of his two Irish point runs in 2024 reads very well and he may yet deliver on that promise under rules.
runners.comment: Tailed off on chase/handicap debut but may yet deliver on his Irish point promise

races.race_name: SWRSL - Dan Ward Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Lemon Leaf
runners.spotlight: Shaped well on his hurdling debut when second in 2m Aintree novice last May; well held after an absence both runs since at Wincanton and Warwick, but could still do better for leading yard now going into handicaps.
runners.comment: Well held last two runs but could still do better for leading yard now going into h'caps

runners.name: Mind Hunter
runners.spotlight: It's now 12 starts since his last success (2022) and he came in a below-par fifth of seven in 2m4f handicap at Leicester in November; others are preferred.
runners.comment: It's now 12 starts since his last success (2022); others are preferred

runners.name: Kellebelle
runners.spotlight: Arrives in good nick since sent handicapping, third of six at Huntingdon (2m4f) in February; not out of things in her bid for a maiden victory.
runners.comment: Arrives in good nick since sent handicapping; not out of things in bid for a maiden win

runners.name: Ferando
runners.spotlight: Looks a low-level maiden and beat only one in 2m3f handicap chase here nine days ago; tongue-strap is refitted here.
runners.comment: Beat only one in 2m3f handicap chase here nine days ago; tongue-strap is refitted here

runners.name: King Berry
runners.spotlight: Modest form in bumpers (for Ed De Giles) and well held in three 2m maiden hurdles this year, beating one at Newbury last month; tongue tied for his handicap debut with much better required.
runners.comment: Well held in three 2m maiden hurdles this year; tongue tied for handicap debut

runners.name: Largy Panic
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo who advanced his form on just his second go in handicap company when scoring at Kempton (2m) 29 days ago; up 2lb but showed a good attitude there and has a big shout with this return to further also a likely plus.
runners.comment: Improved when scoring at Kempton latest; big shout with return to further a likely plus

runners.name: Lancier Des Obeaux
runners.spotlight: Has cut little ice in three maiden hurdles (at up to 2m3f) this year; tongue tied for this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Has cut little ice in three maiden hurdles this year; handicap debutant

runners.name: Yourcardismarked
runners.spotlight: Did not offer much in three maiden hurdles (around 2m) this year but this son of Harzand goes handicapping now and is bred to be better suited by this longer trip; tongue-tie is also added.
runners.comment: Did not offer much in three maiden hurdles; bred to be better suited by this longer trip

runners.name: Could Be You
runners.spotlight: Yet to show any form under rules, pulled up in 2m4f Leicester novice in January on his final run for Ryan Potter; tongue tied on yard/handicap debut and he could still take a step forward given he was placed in Irish points.
runners.comment: Yet to show any form under rules; could still go forward for new yard on h'cap debut

runners.name: Midnight Ginger
runners.spotlight: On a long losing run but she wasn't discredited on the back of wind surgery when fourth of six in 2m3f Warwick handicap 60 days ago; possibilities off a falling mark.
runners.comment: Not discredited when fourth at Warwick 60 days ago; possibilities off a falling mark

races.race_name: SWRSL - Dan Ward Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Cumhacht
runners.spotlight: Yet to score but he comes here on the back of a good second of six in 2m handicap here in February; the winner and third there have both gone in since, so he's a player off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Good second here in February; form franked, so player off same mark in bid for maiden win

runners.name: Jour d'Orage
runners.spotlight: Still to get his head in front and he came in a never-dangerous seventh in 2m4f Wincanton handicap last month; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Never-dangerous seventh in 2m4f Wincanton handicap last month; others appeal more

runners.name: Mancero
runners.spotlight: French hurdles winner; yet to score for his current yard but he wasn't disgraced when fourth of 11 in 2m4f Wincanton handicap 20 days ago given he was set a lot to do; no forlorn hope from a falling mark.
runners.comment: Not disgraced when fourth at Wincanton 20 days ago; no forlorn hope from a falling mark

runners.name: Lion Of The Desert
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden but he failed to stay when ninth in 3m Chepstow handicap 26 days ago; not out of things back in trip.
runners.comment: Still a maiden but he failed to stay in 3m Chepstow handicap 26 days ago; not out of it

runners.name: Royal Rhythm
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2023 and he's been off the track since pulled up in 3m handicap here in November; handily weighted if resurgent after a break.
runners.comment: Off since pulled up here in November; handily weighted if resurgent after a break

runners.name: Spanish Dancer
runners.spotlight: Has been brought along steadily, fifth of ten in 2m Lingfield novice hurdle in February; steps up in trip now going into handicaps and looks capable of better.
runners.comment: Has been brought along steadily; steps up in trip for h'cap debut and capable of better

runners.name: Neeps And Tatties
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 7yo who took a step forward when second of six in C&D handicap last month; she can go well again nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: Step forward when second over C&D last month; she can go well again nudged up 1lb
runners.owner: Dalziel Family, K Rogers, D Vowles

runners.name: Whatmakes Honey B
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a breakthrough win at Taunton (2m3f) but she's offered little this season, a well-held sixth back at Taunton (3m) last month; not easy to make a case for.
runners.comment: Has offered little this term, well-held Taunton sixth latest; not easy to make a case for

runners.name: Forest Spirit
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m4f Flat winner but she's yet to match that form in this sphere, fifth of six in C&D handicap in October; needs to step forward after her absence.
runners.comment: A fair 1m4f Flat winner but yet to match that form in this sphere; needs to step forward
